skill.name: test-fixing
skill.description: Guides agents through running tests, grouping failures by root cause, applying focused fixes, and verifying the full suite passes.
skill.summary: Systematically run, group, fix, and verify failing tests.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command findings are markdown examples for expected test and git commands, not Ruby backtick execution. The weak cryptography alert at SKILL.md:3 is unsupported by the file content. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: External Test Commands Require User Awareness
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run project test commands such as make test, uv run pytest, and git diff. This is expected for a test-fixing workflow, but tests can execute project code in the local environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced commands are legitimate test and git inspection examples, not hidden shell execution. Risk remains because running tests can execute repository code.

content.value_statement: Failing tests can hide several root causes and waste debugging time. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through grouped fixes and focused verification.
actual_capabilities: Runs the full test suite to collect current failures.
Groups failures by error type, module, and likely root cause.
Prioritizes infrastructure, API, and logic issues in a clear order.
Applies minimal code changes that match project conventions.
Runs focused tests after each fix before final verification.
Uses git diff to understand recent changes during debugging.
limitations: Requires a working local test command such as make test or pytest.
Does not define project-specific test commands beyond common examples.
Cannot guarantee coverage quality beyond the existing test suite.
May need human review for broad refactors or ambiguous expected behavior.

use_cases.title: Repair CI Failures
use_cases.description: Group CI test failures and fix the highest impact root causes first.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Refactor Work
use_cases.description: Update imports, signatures, and assertions after a code reorganization.

use_cases.title: Triage Large Test Output
use_cases.description: Turn a long failure log into ordered repair groups and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Current Failures
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with no extra context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the test suite, group the failures, fix the highest impact group first, and verify each fix.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Refactor Regressions
prompt_templates.scenario: Tests started failing after code was moved or renamed.
prompt_templates.prompt: The tests started failing after my refactor. Use the test-fixing workflow to identify broken imports, API changes, and logic regressions.

prompt_templates.title: Target One Test Area
prompt_templates.scenario: A specific module or test folder is failing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Focus on the failing tests in the payments test folder. Group related failures, make minimal fixes, and run focused verification before the full suite.

prompt_templates.title: Recover From CI Output
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using pasted CI failure details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this CI failure output to form root-cause groups. Fix the groups in dependency order, explain each change, and finish with full-suite verification.

output_examples.input: The test suite has several import errors after a module rename.
output_examples.output: The agent reports one root cause group for renamed modules, updates affected imports, runs the focused tests, then runs the full suite.

output_examples.input: Five tests fail because a function signature changed.
output_examples.output: The agent identifies the changed signature, updates callers or test expectations consistently, and verifies the affected files before continuing.

output_examples.input: CI shows many unrelated failures after a merge.
output_examples.output: The agent separates infrastructure, API, and assertion failures, then fixes each group in priority order with verification after every group.

best_practices: Fix one root-cause group at a time and verify it before moving on.
Prefer focused test runs during debugging, then run the complete suite at the end.
Use recent diffs and project conventions to keep fixes small and consistent.
anti_patterns: Do not change tests first when production behavior is clearly broken.
Do not skip full-suite verification after focused tests pass.
Do not mix unrelated refactors into test repair changes.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: It instructs the agent to run project test commands when the environment allows command execution.

faq.question: Can it fix failures from any test framework?
faq.answer: It is framework agnostic, but examples focus on make and pytest workflows.

faq.question: Will it rewrite tests or source code?
faq.answer: It recommends minimal changes based on the diagnosed root cause and project conventions.

faq.question: Is it useful for CI failures?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use CI output to group failures and decide the repair order.

faq.question: Does it handle flaky tests?
faq.answer: It can help identify patterns, but confirming flakiness may require repeated runs and environment review.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the failing test command, recent changes, or CI output when available.
